channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Zuckerberg defends Meta’s Instagram, WhatsApp deals in U.S. antitrust trial
item.description: Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg took the stand on Monday at a high-stakes trial in Washington over U.S. antitrust enforcers’ claims that the company spent billions of dollars to acquire Instagram and WhatsApp to fend off Facebook competitors.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Bankrupt 23andMe faces scrutiny by U.S. House Committee as government seeks testimony from cofounder
item.description: A U.S. House committee on Tuesday asked 23andMe’s cofounder to testify next month as it launched an investigation into the risk of genetic data being transferred to potential buyers amid the DNA testing company’s bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s big plan for better AI is you
item.description: Apple has a brand new plan to make Apple Intelligence smarter. That plan is you — more specifically, your data. A post on the company’s Machine Learning blog explains the plan to begin on-device analysis of your user data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will let players repair their gear, but only if they download an app
item.description: The Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will give players the ability to repair their equipment with a companion app.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google Stops Issuing Android 12 and 12L Updates - Tech Alert
item.description: Google stops providing updates for Android 12 and Android 12L, leaving devices open to security issues. Upgrade your older devices now to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump's fluctuating tariffs stir confusion in tech industry
item.description: The Trump administration’s back-and-forth moves on tariffs for technology products are stirring confusion in a sector heavily reliant on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Meta antitrust trial tests MAGA-tech relationship
item.description: A major antitrust trial against Meta that kicked off Monday — with CEO Mark Zuckerberg taking the stand — is a test of how much tech giants can get out of the MAGA embrace that swept across the industry in the wake of President Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: What is technical debt? A business risk IT must manage
item.description: What is technical debt? Technical debt is the cost accrued over time from technology implementation decisions that emphasize expediency over long-term quality and maintenance.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk and Jack Dorsey want to kill IP law. That would be a huge mistake
item.description: Few statements communicate a sentiment more directly than the four words Jack Dorsey, a cofounder of Twitter, posted over the weekend on the platform he no longer controls: “delete all IP law,” was his simple message.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft releases out-of-band updates to fix reporting error
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency patches to fix an apparent reporting error in Active Directory (AD) Group Policy, which allows administrators to manage and configure user and computer settings in Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Self-driving Tesla hits car, sign while driver sleeps behind wheel
item.description: According to police, the driver was asleep at the wheel and using Tesla's self-driving feature to navigate.
item.dc:creator: Drew Kolek, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok travel influencers are illegally hitching a ride on Mauritania’s Iron Ore Train
item.description: Mauritania isn’t typically a major tourist destination. But its only railway has recently become the subject of a viral TikTok travel trend: riding the “Iron Ore Train.” This 437-mile journey through the Sahara desert offers dramatic selfie backdrops—and no shortage of controversy.
item.dc:creator: Regina Borsellino, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI Isn't One Size Fits All: Federal Agencies Seek Right Tools
item.description: Federal agencies seeking to modernize their IT systems and offer innovative new services should consider using deterministic AI, which offers distinct advantages over generative AI.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence supercomputers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call on National Archives to investigate use of Signal and Gmail by top government officials
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee say the reported use of Signal and Gmail by national security officials creates major cybersecurity risks.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Keysight tools tackle data center deployment efficiency
item.description: Test and performance measurement vendor Keysight Technologies has developed Keysight Artificial Intelligence (KAI) to identify performance inhibitors affecting large GPU deployments. It emulates workload profiles, rather than using actual resources, to pinpoint performance bottlenecks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Now companies can patch Windows 11 without rebooting
item.description: Microsoft has now added support for hotpatching in Windows 11 Enterprise 24H2 on x64 systems (AMD/Intel), Bleeping Computer reports. The support means that some security updates can be installed without restarting the computers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump’s tariff flip-flop: Policy shifts disrupt tech procurement for CIOs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Sunday he will reveal new tariff rates on imported semiconductors this week, the latest in a series of rapid policy shifts that have thrown the technology sector into confusion and forced businesses to reevaluate procurement strategies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Discover Financial Services exec: GenAI isn’t yet ready for prime time
item.description: As generative AI (genAI) tools grow in adoption and sophistication, Fortune 500 companies are finding they can rely more on data to drive decisions and improve operations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Infoblox, Google Cloud partner to protect hybrid and multicloud enterprise resources
item.description: Infoblox and Google Cloud announced a partnership that powers new products from each company that they say will help enterprise organizations accelerate their cloud adoption with advanced networking and security capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cutting-edge drone tech maps land and water with laser accuracy
item.description: New drone technology maps land and water with stunning accuracy, giving researchers and conservationists a new way to understand our planet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort
item.description: OPM said the digital versions of four federal retirement booklets are immediately available for download and should reduce delays for federal retirees. The post OPM digitizes federal retirement booklets as part of longtime modernization effort first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network
